notes: Textures are badly rendered - takes time for it to load - if Esync is on. It's necessary to say that it suffers from a FPS drop when entering new roms or turning the screen too fast

title: BioShock Remastered
notes:
Using launch options: `PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -windowed`
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 is necessary to fix low resolution textures.
A grey transparent box/seam is visible during gameplay but is easily ignored.

notes: launch options: "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%"
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Was able to run perfectly after setting "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -windowed" launch option.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX 580 Series (POLARIS10, DRM 3.27.0, 4.19.0-041900rc4-generic, LLVM 6.0.0)
notes:
Ryzen 1700x / RTX 580 (8GB)

PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% allows it to run, but it runs at about 20fps. I tried adding PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 (as some people said it made the game work for them) but it did not help FPS issues.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX 580 Series (POLARIS10, DRM 3.27.0, 4.19.0-041900rc4-generic, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes: Same poster as just below this. I got it working a great framerate after doing a lot of updates, I'm not sure what fixed it, but maybe updating to the develop branch of MESA. WINED3D had to be set to 0 for it to fix the lag though.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Worked perfectly two days ago with no options. Tried no ESYNC but no luck. Opening any menu like options now locks up the game. Same status for release and beta.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Needs "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%", otherwise runs with  low resolution textures. Even with Esync disable, the game stutters frequently.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Using the following launch options string "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro'. Disabling Esync is required to not have blocky textures. Framedrops sometimes.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Runs perfectly straight 'out of the box' with no tweaking or launch options required. Played for three hours, no crashes or bugs whatsoever.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes:
I set launch options: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro 
The textures are blurry without this setting. Other then that the game runs flawlessly.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Runs out of the box without any tweaks. Stutters sometimes. Hacking screens take ages to load. After tabbing out and back in FPS are limited to 11. Overall perfectly playable, runs with 60 FPS on highest settings most of the time.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes:
Use the following launch option otherwise textures don't load in properly and are very blocky. There is occasional stuttering, but is completely playable.

PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro

notes: Runs great.

notes: Hacking screens take ages to load
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: I thought that the textures were normal for this old game so I was going to give platinum. After see the comments here I have disabled Esync and now I must admit that it looks much better!
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes:
Crashed abruptly while playing the introductory section with default settings.

PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%
makes the game work perfectly.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Game starts, but won't make it past first loading screen
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Attempting to launch the game from Steam with no special options causes it to stop running after the pop-up banner appears, and even that displays as just a solid black box. Enabling WINED3D causes no visible change. Disabling esync or D3D11 causes an instant crash before the banner can even appear, so I have not bothered to try a combination of the three.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: The first part of the game runs fine but attempting to hack anything (which is needed to proceed with the main story) causes a freeze.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Runs out of the box, but with low resolution textures. Starting with "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro" fixed this issue and some issues with ultrawide resolutions. After this tweak, 60 fps all the way without any stutters.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: work without tweak.
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes:
Textures take a LONG time to load at a proper quality (5-10 minutes per texture)
Certain actions cause the game to freeze for a long time (clicking save, opening brightness menu)
Game gets lag spikes when entering new areas and freezes for a few seconds
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Runs perfectly our of the box with latest Proton version 3.16-5, just minor graphical glitch is low resolution (blurry) textures. To fix that you need to set launch options to: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% -nointro (this will also skip the intro of the game), then game loads high resolution textures and continues to run perfectly. I even unlock few achievements on last session ;)
title: BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Now runs out of the box without launch options with Proton 3-16.6. Textures are still very low res. Saving takes a very long time (I thought the game froze).
title: BioShock™ Remastered
BioShock™ Remastered

notes: Takes a long time to switch from game to "hacking" mini-game; * During which the sound freezes (get stuck in a loop, playing the same thing over and over); * Sometimes slows down; * It is possible to alt-tab between Bioshock and other applications. Without PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% the hacking minigames takes a long time to open. Freezes in random zones but is totally playable
